info.name: GreenThumb API
info.description:
Complete API documentation for the GreenThumb houseplant management platform.

## Setup
1. Import this collection into Postman
2. Set the `base_url` variable to `http://localhost:8080`
3. Follow the **Demo Flow** folder in order — it walks through the full user journey from registration to care logging

## Variables
- `base_url` — Spring Boot server URL (default: http://localhost:8080)
- `token` — JWT token, set automatically by the Login request
- `plant_id` — set manually after adding a plant
- `species_id` — set manually after browsing species
- `schedule_id` — set manually after creating a schedule
- `review_id` — set manually after writing a review

## Auth
All endpoints except Auth and public GET /api/species require the `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}` header. This is pre-configured on every request in this collection.

item.name: 🌱 Demo Flow (Run In Order)

request.description:
Registers a new user. Returns 201. A verification email is sent.

⚠️ In dev mode (H2), email is sent to Gmail — check your inbox or use the seeded user below to skip verification.

**Seeded users (skip Steps 1-2):**
- hamza@greenthumb.com / User@1234
- sara@greenthumb.com / User@1234
- admin@greenthumb.com / Admin@1234

item.name: Step 2 — Verify email (copy token from email)

request.description:
Activates the account. Paste the token from the verification email into the query param.

If using a seeded user, skip this step — seeded accounts are pre-verified.

Authenticates the user. The test script automatically saves the JWT token to the `token` collection variable. All subsequent requests will use it.

Expected response:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "token": "eyJ...",
    "userId": 2,
    "name": "Hamza Ali",
    "email": "hamza@greenthumb.com",
    "role": "USER"
  }
}
```

item.name: Step 4 — Browse species (pick a species ID)

request.description:
Lists all species in the catalogue. No auth required.

Note the `id` of a species you want to add — you'll use it in Step 5.

Seeded species IDs (approximate — check actual response):
- 1: Peace Lily
- 2: Snake Plant
- 3: Spider Plant
- 4: Phalaenopsis Orchid
- 5: Pothos
- 6: Aloe Vera

body.raw:
{
  "speciesId": 4,
  "nickname": "Orchie",
  "location": "Living Room Windowsill",
  "acquiredDate": "2025-03-15",
  "notes": "Got this as a gift. Blooming beautifully."
}

request.description:
Adds a plant to the authenticated user's collection. Change `speciesId` to the ID you noted in Step 4.

The test script saves the returned `plant_id` automatically.

Creates a recurring watering schedule for the plant.

Care types: `WATER`, `FERTILISE`, `REPOT`, `PRUNE`, `MIST`

Set `nextDueDate` to today or earlier to make it appear in the due-today list.

Returns all plants with a care schedule where `nextDueDate <= today`. This is the dashboard feed.

If you set `nextDueDate` in the past or as today in Step 6, your plant will appear here.

Records a care action. This also advances the matching schedule's `nextDueDate` forward by `intervalDays`.

After logging, re-run Step 7 — the plant will no longer appear in due-today.

item.name: Step 9 — Get care tips from Perenual

request.description:
Fetches watering, sunlight, and toxicity tips from the Perenual API. Results are cached for 7 days — the response includes a `fromCache` flag.

Set `{{species_id}}` to any species ID from Step 4.

Writes a review for the species linked to this plant. One review per plant per user is enforced.

Rating: 1–5 (required). Body: optional, max 1000 characters.

item.description: Complete end-to-end walkthrough of the GreenThumb API. Run each request in sequence. This is the recommended way to test the API for the first time.

item.name: 🔐 Auth

request.description:
Creates a new user account with PENDING status. Sends a verification email via Gmail SMTP.

**Validation rules:**
- `name`: 2–100 characters, required
- `email`: valid format, must be unique
- `password`: min 8 characters

**Returns:** 201 Created

**Errors:**
- 400: validation failure
- 409: email already registered

response.name: 201 Created

originalRequest.description:
Creates a new user account with PENDING status. Sends a verification email via Gmail SMTP.

**Validation rules:**
- `name`: 2–100 characters, required
- `email`: valid format, must be unique
- `password`: min 8 characters

**Returns:** 201 Created

**Errors:**
- 400: validation failure
- 409: email already registered

Creates a new user account with PENDING status. Sends a verification email via Gmail SMTP.

**Validation rules:**
- `name`: 2–100 characters, required
- `email`: valid format, must be unique
- `password`: min 8 characters

**Returns:** 201 Created

**Errors:**
- 400: validation failure
- 409: email already registered

request.description:
Activates the user account by consuming the email verification token.

Token is a UUID sent in the verification email link. Expires after 24 hours. Single use.

**Returns:** 200 OK

**Errors:**
- 400: token invalid / expired / already used

exec: console.log('✅ Token saved to collection variable');

request.description:
Authenticates a user and returns a JWT. The test script saves the token automatically to `{{token}}`.

The token is valid for 24 hours (86400000ms as configured).

**Pre-conditions:** Account must be email-verified and status ACTIVE.

**Returns:** 200 OK with JWT and user details

**Errors:**
- 401: wrong credentials
- 400: email not verified

originalRequest.description:
Authenticates a user and returns a JWT. The test script saves the token automatically to `{{token}}`.

The token is valid for 24 hours (86400000ms as configured).

**Pre-conditions:** Account must be email-verified and status ACTIVE.

**Returns:** 200 OK with JWT and user details

**Errors:**
- 401: wrong credentials
- 400: email not verified

request.description:
Login as the seeded admin user. Required before using any /api/admin/* or /api/species POST/PUT/DELETE endpoints.

Saves admin JWT to `{{token}}`.

Sends a password reset link to the given email. Always returns 200 regardless of whether the email exists — prevents user enumeration.

Reset token expires after 1 hour.

Completes the password reset. The token comes from the reset email link. Single use, expires in 1 hour.

**Errors:**
- 400: token invalid / expired / already used

item.description: Public authentication endpoints. No JWT required.

item.name: 👤 User Profile

request.description:
Returns the authenticated user's full profile.

**Returns:** 200 with id, name, email, role, status, profilePictureUrl, bio, location, createdAt

Updates name, bio, and location for the authenticated user.

**Validation:** name is required (2–100 chars). bio max 300 chars.

**Returns:** 200 with updated profile

Uploads a new profile picture. Accepts image files (jpg, png, webp etc.). Uploaded to Cloudinary under `greenthumb/profiles/`.

**Body:** multipart/form-data with key `file`

**Returns:** 200 with updated profile including new `profilePictureUrl`

Changes the authenticated user's password. Verifies the current password before allowing the update.

**Validation:** newPassword min 8 characters.

**Errors:**
- 400: current password is incorrect

item.description: Authenticated user profile management. All endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`.

item.name: 🌿 Species Catalogue

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of all species. No auth required.

**Query params:**
- `page`: page number (0-based)
- `size`: items per page
- `sort`: field,direction (e.g. `commonName,asc`)
- `query`: optional search term (searches commonName and scientificName)

Searches species by common name or scientific name. Case-insensitive partial match.

Examples: `peace`, `snake`, `Spathiphyllum`, `aloe`

Returns full details of a single species including toxicToCats, wateringFrequencyDays, lightRequirement, and imageUrl.

**Errors:**
- 404: species not found

Returns all user reviews for a given species. Public endpoint.

Includes rating, body, reviewer name, and timestamp.

Fetches care tips from the Perenual Plant API for the given species. Results are cached locally for 7 days.

Response includes:
- `wateringTip`: watering guidance
- `sunlightTip`: light requirements
- `toxicityNote`: cat safety warning
- `fromCache`: true if served from local cache

**Requires:** USER or ADMIN role

Creates a new species in the catalogue. Requires ADMIN role.

**Login as admin first:** use the `Login (Admin)` request.

**Validation:** commonName is required. wateringFrequencyDays must be positive.

**Returns:** 201 Created with species data

Updates an existing species. Requires ADMIN role.

All fields in the body are used to replace the current values.

Uploads a reference image for a species. Requires ADMIN role.

**Body:** multipart/form-data with key `file`

Image uploaded to Cloudinary under `greenthumb/species/`

Hard-deletes a species from the catalogue. Requires ADMIN role.

⚠️ This will fail if users have plants linked to this species (foreign key constraint). Delete user plants first.

item.description: Plant species catalogue. GET endpoints are public. POST/PUT/DELETE require ADMIN role.

item.name: 🪴 My Plants

request.description:
Returns the authenticated user's plant collection with pagination. Each plant includes embedded species data.

Response includes: nickname, location, status, photoUrl, acquiredDate, species details.

Returns all plants with at least one active care schedule where `nextDueDate <= today`. This powers the dashboard's due-today widget.

Returns a list (not paginated). Plants appear here until care is logged.

Returns details of a specific plant. Ownership is validated — you can only view your own plants.

**Errors:**
- 404: plant not found or not owned by you

body.raw:
{
  "speciesId": 4,
  "nickname": "Orchie",
  "location": "Living Room Windowsill",
  "acquiredDate": "2025-03-15",
  "notes": "Got this as a gift. Blooming beautifully."
}

request.description:
Adds a plant to the collection. The `speciesId` must match a species in the catalogue.

**Required:** speciesId, nickname
**Optional:** location, acquiredDate (YYYY-MM-DD), notes

**Returns:** 201 Created. Test script saves the plant ID.

Updates a plant's nickname, location, notes, and health status.

**Status values:** `HEALTHY`, `STRUGGLING`, `DEAD`

Ownership is validated — you can only update your own plants.

Uploads a photo for a specific plant. Uploaded to Cloudinary under `greenthumb/plants/`.

**Body:** multipart/form-data with key `file`

Returns updated plant with new `photoUrl`.

Removes a plant from the collection. Cascades to care schedules, care logs, and reviews linked to this plant.

Ownership validated — you can only delete your own plants.

item.description: User's personal plant collection. All endpoints require authentication.

item.name: 📅 Care Schedules

request.description: Returns all care schedules for a plant. Each schedule has a `careType`, `intervalDays`, `nextDueDate`, and `isActive` flag.

Creates a recurring schedule for a specific care type.

**Care types:** `WATER`, `FERTILISE`, `REPOT`, `PRUNE`, `MIST`

**Rules:**
- Only one schedule per care type per plant
- intervalDays must be positive
- nextDueDate is required (YYYY-MM-DD)

**Errors:**
- 400: duplicate care type for this plant
- 404: plant not found or not owned
Updates the interval and next due date for an existing schedule. Ownership validated.
Soft-deactivates a schedule (sets isActive=false). The schedule record is kept for historical reference. Ownership validated.

item.description: Recurring care schedule management per plant. All endpoints require authentication.

item.name: 📋 Care Logs

request.description:
Records a care action for a plant. Two effects:
1. Creates a CareLog entry with timestamp and notes
2. Advances the matching active CareSchedule's `nextDueDate` by `intervalDays`

**Care types:** `WATER`, `FERTILISE`, `REPOT`, `PRUNE`, `MIST`

Notes are optional. After logging, the plant will no longer appear in due-today (until the next due date is reached).

Returns the full care history for a plant, ordered most recent first.

Includes: careType, performedAt, notes, performedByName.

item.description: Record and view care actions performed on plants.

item.name: ⭐ Reviews

request.description:
Writes a review for the species linked to this plant.

**Rules:**
- One review per plant per user (enforced)
- Rating: 1–5 (required)
- Body: optional, max 1000 characters

**Errors:**
- 400: already reviewed this plant
- 404: plant not found or not owned

Updates your own review. Only the original reviewer can update.

**Errors:**
- 400: you can only edit your own reviews
- 404: review not found

Deletes your own review. Only the original reviewer can delete.

**Errors:**
- 400: you can only delete your own reviews

item.description: Plant species reviews written through a user's personal plant.

item.name: 🛡️ Admin Panel

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of all registered users with their role, status, and basic details.

**Requires:** ADMIN role

Returns: id, name, email, role, status, createdAt for each user.

Sets a user's status to INACTIVE (soft delete). The record is preserved.

The deactivated user can no longer log in — `isAccountNonLocked()` returns false for INACTIVE status.

**Requires:** ADMIN role

**Errors:**
- 404: user not found

Returns aggregated platform statistics.

**Response includes:**
- `totalUsers`: total registered accounts
- `totalPlants`: total plants across all users
- `careLogsThisWeek`: care actions logged in the last 7 days
- `topSpecies`: top 5 species by plant count (id, commonName, plantCount)
- `plantsByStatus`: count breakdown by HEALTHY / STRUGGLING / DEAD

**Requires:** ADMIN role

originalRequest.description:
Returns aggregated platform statistics.

**Response includes:**
- `totalUsers`: total registered accounts
- `totalPlants`: total plants across all users
- `careLogsThisWeek`: care actions logged in the last 7 days
- `topSpecies`: top 5 species by plant count (id, commonName, plantCount)
- `plantsByStatus`: count breakdown by HEALTHY / STRUGGLING / DEAD

**Requires:** ADMIN role

item.description: Admin-only endpoints. Login as admin first using `Login (Admin)` in the Auth folder.

item.name: 🧪 Testing Flows

request.description:
**Expected:** 409 Conflict

Register with an already-used email. Should return:
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Email is already registered: hamza@greenthumb.com"
}
```

**Expected:** 401 Unauthorized

Attempts login with incorrect credentials. Should return:
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Invalid email or password"
}
```

item.name: Test: Access protected endpoint without token

request.description:
**Expected:** 401 Unauthorized

Attempts to access a protected endpoint without a Bearer token.

**Expected:** 403 Forbidden

Login as a regular USER first (not admin), then run this. Should return:
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Access denied: insufficient permissions"
}
```

item.name: Test: Add plant with invalid species ID

request.description:
**Expected:** 404 Not Found

Attempts to add a plant linked to a non-existent species ID.

**Expected:** 400 Bad Request

Run this after already creating a WATER schedule for the same plant. Should return:
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "A WATER schedule already exists for this plant."
}
```

**Expected:** 400 Bad Request

Run after already writing a review for the same plant. Should return:
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "You have already reviewed this plant."
}
```

**Expected:** 400 Bad Request (validation)

Rating must be 1–5. Should return validation error:
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Validation failed: {rating=Rating must not exceed 5}"
}
```

item.name: Test: Change password with wrong current

request.description:
**Expected:** 400 Bad Request

Should return:
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Current password is incorrect"
}
```

**Expected:** 200 OK (even for unknown email)

Prevents user enumeration. The response is always the same regardless of whether the email exists:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "If that email is registered, a reset link has been sent."
}
```

item.description: Isolated test scenarios for validating specific API behaviours. Run these to confirm edge cases and error handling.
